enonce.texte:
Read the text below about recycling and answer the questions that follow.

How to Recycle Plastic Bottles

Recycling is the process of turning used materials into new products. It helps protect our environment. Here is a simple procedure for recycling plastic bottles:

  1. Collect empty plastic bottles from your home or school.
  2. Rinse the bottles with water to remove any dirt or liquid.
  3. Remove the labels and caps from the bottles.
  4. Sort the bottles by colour (clear, green, brown).
  5. Take the bottles to a recycling centre near you.
  6. Recycle them so that they can be turned into new products.

As a result of recycling, we reduce waste and save energy. Therefore, it is important to recycle every day. Consequently, our environment stays cleaner and healthier.
images: A simple illustration showing the steps of recycling plastic bottles: collecting, rinsing, removing labels, sorting by colour, and taking to a recycling centre.

questions.texte: What is recycling? Define it in your own words.
questions.reponse: Recycling is the process of turning used materials into new products to protect the environment.

questions.texte: List the steps for recycling plastic bottles in order.
questions.reponse:
1. Collect empty plastic bottles
2. Rinse the bottles with water
3. Remove the labels and caps
4. Sort the bottles by colour
5. Take the bottles to a recycling centre
6. Recycle them into new products

questions.texte: Why is recycling important? Use a cause and effect connector in your answer.
questions.reponse: Recycling is important because it reduces waste and saves energy. As a result, our environment stays cleaner and healthier.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences using the correct cause and effect connector: as a result, therefore, consequently, so that.

questions.texte: We recycle plastic bottles, __________ we reduce waste in our environment.
She sorted the bottles correctly, __________ they could be recycled easily.
Many people do not recycle their waste. __________, our environment is polluted.
They collected used items from the neighbourhood. __________, they were able to start a recycling program.
We should recycle paper __________ we can save trees.

intitule: Writing: A Short Text on Recycling

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) explaining how to recycle used items at home or at school. Use at least two cause and effect connectors (as a result, therefore, consequently, so that) in your paragraph.
images: A simple illustration showing recycling bins for different materials: paper, plastic, glass, and organic waste.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph explaining how to recycle used items at home or at school. Use at least two cause and effect connectors.
questions.reponse:
This is a creative writing task. Example response:
Recycling is an important activity that we can do at school. First, we should collect used paper, plastic bottles, and cans. Then, we sort them into different bins. After that, we take them to the recycling centre. Therefore, we help reduce waste and save resources. As a result, our environment becomes cleaner and healthier. We should also encourage others to recycle so that we can all enjoy a better future.

enonce.texte:
Read the text below about recycling paper. Then, complete the sentences with the correct cause and effect connectors.

Recycling Paper

Recycling paper is a simple process that helps save trees. First, we collect old newspapers, magazines, and cardboard. Then, we put them in a recycling bin. The paper is taken to a factory where it is cleaned and turned into pulp. This pulp is then made into new paper. __________, we save millions of trees every year. __________, recycling paper reduces the amount of waste in landfills. We should all recycle paper __________ we can protect our forests.
images: A simple illustration showing the paper recycling process: collecting old paper, cleaning, turning into pulp, and making new paper.

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct connector: as a result, consequently, so that.
questions.reponse:
1. As a result, we save millions of trees every year.
2. Consequently, recycling paper reduces the amount of waste in landfills.
3. We should all recycle paper so that we can protect our forests.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the main benefit of recycling paper mentioned in the text?
sousQuestions.reponse: The main benefit is saving trees and protecting forests.

enonce.texte: Match the words on the left with their definitions on the right. Write the correct letter next to each number.
images: A simple illustration showing recycling symbols and icons for different recyclable materials.

questions.texte: Match the words with their definitions:

sousQuestions.reponse: e. To turn used materials into new products
d. To separate items by type
c. The natural world around us

intitule: Creative Writing: Recycling at My School

enonce.texte: Imagine you are in charge of a recycling program at your school. Write a short text (50-80 words) explaining what you will do and why it is important. Use cause and effect connectors in your writing.
images: An illustration showing school children placing paper and plastic bottles into clearly marked recycling bins in a schoolyard.

questions.texte: Write a short text about starting a recycling program at your school. Include cause and effect connectors.
questions.reponse:
This is a creative writing task. Example response:
I will start a recycling program at my school. First, I will put recycling bins in every classroom. Then, I will teach students how to sort paper and plastic. Therefore, we will reduce waste and keep our school clean. As a result, our school will be a better place to learn.
